try_module_3_sketch0.title: Rename Assignments to Roles
try_module_3_sketch0.prompt: The volunteer project now uses `roles.txt` as the clearer name for its assignment list. Rename tracked `assignments.txt` to `roles.txt` with Git so the path change is staged.
try_module_3_sketch0.hint: Use the Git-aware move for the tracked file; no new folder is needed.

help.concept: The Module 3 project reuses the rename skill in the same cumulative repository.

try_module_3_sketch0.starterCode:
Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
try_module_3_sketch0.instructions: The volunteer project now uses `roles.txt` as the clearer name for its assignment list. Rename tracked `assignments.txt` to `roles.txt` with Git so the path change is staged.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Old volunteer notes

Call list from last season.
' > volunteer-project/old-notes.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Start volunteer project'
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Update volunteer assignments'
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 9:30 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Update volunteer meeting time'

try_module_3_sketch1.title: Remove the Obsolete Volunteer Notes
try_module_3_sketch1.prompt: The old call list is no longer part of the project. Remove tracked `old-notes.txt` with Git, then inspect the staged name-status summary together with the rename.
try_module_3_sketch1.hint: Stage the tracked removal and review both structural changes.

help.concept: The rename and removal serve one release-cleanup purpose.
help.hint_1: Use git rm for the tracked note.
help.hint_2: Preserve README.md, meeting-times.txt, and the renamed roles file.

try_module_3_sketch1.starterCode:
Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
try_module_3_sketch1.instructions: The old call list is no longer part of the project. Remove tracked `old-notes.txt` with Git, then inspect the staged name-status summary together with the rename.

try_module_3_sketch2.title: Create the Volunteer Ignore Policy
try_module_3_sketch2.prompt: Create `volunteer-project/.gitignore`. Starting on line 1, enter exactly `generated/` and `*.log`, one rule per line. Save it, create `generated/report.txt` and `debug.log`, then inspect status with ignored paths included.
try_module_3_sketch2.hint: The generated report and log should be present locally but classified as ignored.

help.concept: The edit is supplied exactly; the Git decision is how the ignore policy joins the release snapshot.
help.hint_1: Use exactly generated/ and *.log on separate lines.
help.hint_2: The structural cleanup must stay staged.

try_module_3_sketch2.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_3_sketch2.solutionCode:
mkdir -p generated
touch generated/report.txt
touch debug.log
git status --short --ignored
try_module_3_sketch2.instructions: Create `volunteer-project/.gitignore`. Starting on line 1, enter exactly `generated/` and `*.log`, one rule per line. Save it, create `generated/report.txt` and `debug.log`, then inspect status with ignored paths included.

try_module_3_sketch3.title: Stage the Volunteer Ignore Policy
try_module_3_sketch3.prompt: Confirm that `generated/report.txt` and `debug.log` are ignored, then stage only `.gitignore` so the policy joins the tracked cleanup.
try_module_3_sketch3.hint: The ignored files stay outside the proposed snapshot; the policy itself is tracked.

help.concept: Ignored output can stay on disk without entering the tracked release snapshot.
help.hint_1: Do not stage the generated files.
help.hint_2: The .gitignore file itself should remain trackable.

try_module_3_sketch3.instructions: Confirm that `generated/report.txt` and `debug.log` are ignored, then stage only `.gitignore` so the policy joins the tracked cleanup.

Training desk: Riley

mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'generated/
*.log

git -C volunteer-project add -A
git -C volunteer-project restore --staged .gitignore

try_module_3_sketch4.title: Recover an Accidental README Rewrite
try_module_3_sketch4.prompt: Open `volunteer-project/README.md`. Find `Assignments and meeting times.` and replace it with `Accidental release rewrite.` Save the file. Stage the mistake, unstage it without losing the working copy, then restore the committed README. Finally inspect the staged name-status summary; only the intended cleanup should remain.
try_module_3_sketch4.hint: Recover the staged layer first, then the working-tree layer.

help.concept: Safe layer-specific restore preserves the intended rename, removal, and ignore policy.
help.hint_1: Unstage README.md before restoring the working copy.

try_module_3_sketch4.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_3_sketch4.solutionCode:
git add README.md
git restore --staged README.md
git restore README.md
git diff --staged --name-status
try_module_3_sketch4.instructions: Open `volunteer-project/README.md`. Find `Assignments and meeting times.` and replace it with `Accidental release rewrite.` Save the file. Stage the mistake, unstage it without losing the working copy, then restore the committed README. Finally inspect the staged name-status summary; only the intended cleanup should remain.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Old volunteer notes

Call list from last season.
' > volunteer-project/old-notes.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Start volunteer project'
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Update volunteer assignments'
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 9:30 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Update volunteer meeting time'
rm -f -- volunteer-project/assignments.txt
rm -f -- volunteer-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
' > volunteer-project/roles.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'generated/
*.log
' > volunteer-project/.gitignore
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project/generated
printf %s '' > volunteer-project/generated/report.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '' > volunteer-project/debug.log
git -C volunteer-project add -A

intro-sketch.title: Finish the Volunteer Project Cleanly
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown: The same small repository now carries Modules 1 and 2. Rename one tracked text file, remove one obsolete note, add exact ignore rules, recover one supplied README mistake, and record one final maintenance snapshot.
